canto_title: The Encounter with Forese and the Shadows of Limbo

scenes.summary: Dante's father urges him to move on as time is of the essence.
Dante follows a group of wise men with ease.
Dante hears weeping and asks the identity of the passing shadows.
A group of ghostly, hollow-eyed souls passes Dante.
Dante compares the souls to Erysichthon and contemplates the fall of Jerusalem.
Dante describes the souls' hollow eyes and the tempting smell of fruit and water.
A shadow approaches and Dante recognizes his old friend Forese.
Forese asks Dante to reveal the identities of his two companions.
Dante expresses his sorrow at seeing Forese's withered state.
Forese explains how the scent of nature induces unquenchable hunger and thirst.
Dante wonders how Forese reached Limbo so quickly after death.
Forese explains how his wife's prayers facilitated his arrival in Limbo.
Forese predicts a future of immodesty in Florentine women.
Dante describes the nature and presence of his two companions.
Dante speaks of his eventual journey toward Beatrice.
Dante identifies Virgil and the shadow of the boatman.
